vulnerability.reference: Warehouse workers lack training to anticipate or respond to unsafe robotic behaviors from learned policies.
vulnerability.generated: Robotic manipulation policies lack embedded physical reasoning to filter implausible synthetic interactions, enabling adoption of unsafe behaviors.

impact.reference: Worker injuries from unexpected robotic movements during rare-object handling.
impact.generated: Physical injury to warehouse workers and damage to equipment from robots executing unsafe manipulation actions.

dose_response.reference: Physically implausible synthetic interactions cause unsafe policy execution, which in shared workspaces exposes warehouse workers and equipment to injuries and operational shutdowns.
dose_response.generated: Diffusion models with long-tail guidance produce out-of-distribution tail-class samples, through exposure of warehouse workers and equipment alongside robotic systems, triggers manipulation policies lacking physical reasoning to filter implausible synthetic interactions, leading to physical injury and equipment damage.
